commands.name: apply_work_item_create
commands.idempotency: exact replay creates no fact and consumes no quota
special_invariants: maximum one successful create
origin.kind must be manual
origin.ref must match allowed prefix
normalized Create digest must match synthetic Fixture contract
create and Lease Work Item binding share one transaction
after Lease binding, an exact replay of the successful Create is resolved before new-create scope rejection and returns the bound Work Item without fact, quota or Lease Event

commands.name: apply_legacy_work_item_import
commands.reason: historical import is outside the synthetic Authoritative Canary

commands.name: add_task_version
commands.idempotency: source_event_key replay creates no fact and consumes no quota
special_invariants: maximum two Task Versions including the initial version
payload digest must match the next ordered synthetic Task Version digest
current lifecycle rules still apply

commands.name: create_execution_attempt
commands.idempotency: source_event_key replay creates no fact and consumes no quota
special_invariants: maximum two Runtime Attempts
objective and Task Version must match the next synthetic Fixture slot
submitted and terminal Work Items remain ineligible

commands.name: bind_historical_execution_attempt
commands.reason: historical/reference-only execution is outside the live Canary scope

commands.name: complete_execution_attempt

quota_effect.artifacts: count every nested Artifact newly inserted by completion

commands.idempotency: source_event_key replay creates no fact and consumes no quota
special_invariants: nested Artifacts share the same transaction and global Artifact quota
all nested Artifact URIs, kinds and digests match the synthetic Fixture policy
Historical Attempts never become dispatchable or completable

commands.name: register_artifact_reference
commands.idempotency: source_event_key replay creates no fact and consumes no quota
special_invariants: maximum four total Artifacts across direct and nested registration
file URI resolves below the exact Fixture root and kind is allowlisted
digest and terminal-state checks remain mandatory

commands.name: record_review_decision
commands.idempotency: source_event_key replay creates no fact and consumes no quota
special_invariants: maximum four Decisions
action and Task Version match the synthetic lifecycle scenario
trusted Principal and policy-derived authority remain mandatory

commands.name: apply_work_item_transition

quota_effect.applied_gate_events: increment only for new transition_applied facts
quota_effect.rejected_gate_events: increment only for new transition_rejected facts
quota_effect.gate_events_total: increment for every new Gate Event
quota_effect.derived_facts: Audit, Outbox and Acceptance Manifest remain transactionally bounded

commands.idempotency: preview/idempotency replay creates no fact and consumes no quota
special_invariants: maximum eight applied Gate Events
maximum eight rejected Gate Events
maximum sixteen total Gate Events
transition and bound evidence IDs match the synthetic lifecycle scenario
Gate, Work Item CAS, Lease usage, Audit, Outbox and Acceptance Manifest share one transaction

commands.name: apply_blocker
commands.reason: changes effective Gate conditions and is unnecessary for the initial Canary

commands.name: clear_blocker
commands.reason: changes effective Gate conditions and is unnecessary for the initial Canary

commands.name: create_delivery_receipt
commands.reason: Delivery is outside Phase 3 synthetic Gate activation scope

commands.name: retry_delivery
commands.reason: Delivery is outside Phase 3 synthetic Gate activation scope

commands.name: acknowledge_delivery
commands.reason: Delivery is outside Phase 3 synthetic Gate activation scope

commands.name: record_outbox_delivery_result
commands.reason: Outbox dispatch mutation is outside the Canary endpoint

commands.name: recover_outbox_event
commands.reason: Outbox recovery is a separately authorized control-plane operation

global_invariants: all allowed writes require the exact active Lease
all allowed writes require a sealed Token-authenticated request context bound to the exact Lease, authorization, current process and persisted Listener attestation
a trusted Principal without that request context is insufficient
all denied and unknown commands fail before domain mutation
trusted Principal context is required for every allowed write
authorization and quota fields are never accepted from command bodies
every normalized command matches the Lease-bound synthetic Fixture contract
actual bounded and denied-table counts reconcile with Lease usage before every write
a newly committed command creates at most one Lease Event and exact replay creates none
idempotency resolution and quota accounting share the domain transaction
